political.s: Apple does not operate a PAC and states it makes no direct contributions to individual candidates or parties, though employee-level FEC data shows a slight right lean ($17K of $25K raised going to Republicans per verified government data). Apple spent $7.82M lobbying federally in 2024 and $10M in 2025, engaging registered advocates in 47 states on issues including antitrust and trade policy. The company's CEO Tim Cook personally donated $1M to Trump's 2026 inauguration, adding a pragmatic bipartisan dimension to an otherwise left-leaning employee donor base.

charity.s: Apple's Employee Giving Program has raised over $880M for nearly 44,000 organizations globally, with Apple matching every employee dollar donated and every hour volunteered. The company has committed over $200M through its Racial Equity and Justice Initiative to education, criminal justice, and economic growth organizations, with more than 200 education grant partners. Apple also funds the (PRODUCT)RED campaign, which has raised over $250M to fight AIDS globally.
charity.focus: Racial equity, education, housing, disaster relief, HIV/AIDS

environment.s: Apple has reduced its overall greenhouse gas emissions by more than 60% since 2015 and is on track for its 'Apple 2030' goal to be carbon neutral across its entire value chain by 2030. All of Apple's corporate facilities worldwide run on 100% renewable energy, and over 320 suppliers representing 95% of direct manufacturing spend have committed to clean energy. Apple's Zero Waste program diverted approximately 600,000 metric tons of waste from landfills in 2024, and the company holds SBTi-validated emissions targets.

labor.s: Apple has faced multiple NLRB complaints in 2024, including charges of requiring employees to sign illegal confidentiality and non-compete agreements, suppressing pay-equity discussions on Slack, and intimidating workers over union activity at retail stores. The NLRB formally ruled in May 2024 that Apple illegally interrogated an employee about union activities and unlawfully barred distribution of union flyers at its World Trade Center store. Only 2 of Apple's 274 U.S. retail stores have unionized, and workers and unions have filed more than two dozen NLRB complaints alleging a range of unlawful anti-union conduct.

dei.s: Apple has maintained strong DEI commitments, with shareholders voting 97% to reject a proposal to eliminate DEI policies at its February 2025 meeting, bucking a broader corporate trend of rolling back DEI programs. The company has sustained gender pay equity since 2017, operates Diversity Network Associations, and invested over $200M in its Racial Equity and Justice Initiative. Apple received a perfect score of 100 on the HRC Corporate Equality Index and was DEI Workplace Certified, reaffirming its stance even amid Trump administration pressure.

animals.s: PETA named Apple its 2023 Company of the Year for eliminating leather from all product lines as part of its carbon-neutral goals, replacing it with recycled FineWoven materials. As a consumer electronics company, Apple does not manufacture cosmetics or pharmaceuticals requiring animal testing, and it does not appear on PETA's list of companies that test on animals. Apple is broadly regarded as cruelty-free in the context of its product category.

guns.s: Apple is a consumer electronics and software company and does not sell, manufacture, or distribute firearms or ammunition. The company has no known business involvement in the firearms industry. Apple has removed certain gun-related apps from its App Store that violated its guidelines.

privacy.s: Apple has consistently ranked among the top companies for user privacy, achieving perfect or near-perfect scores in the EFF's 'Who Has Your Back' report for demanding warrants before sharing user data, publishing transparency reports, and opposing government backdoors. Apple's privacy policy was revised in March 2024 to give users better control over personal data collection, and the company has mandated detailed privacy labels for all App Store apps since May 2024. However, Apple faces ongoing litigation over first-party app data collection practices, and the Ranking Digital Rights index flagged that Apple retracted some previous disclosures about security audits.

execPay.s: Apple CEO Tim Cook received $74.6M in total compensation for fiscal year 2024, an 18% increase from the prior year, driven primarily by $58.1M in stock awards. Apple's 2025 proxy filing disclosed a CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio of 533:1, with the median Apple employee earning $139,483 annually. While Cook's pay is below the most extreme CEO packages, the ratio remains well above typical peer benchmarks and has drawn criticism from labor advocates.

fec.committeeName: US APPLE ASSOCIATION POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE; AKA USAPPLE PAC

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
